items.title: Indira files committal application against office of IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: M. Indira Gandhi has filed a committal application against the office of the Inspector-General of Police for contempt of court, accusing the police of failing to arrest her ex-husband and locate her abducted daughter for over a decade. The move stems from a 2014 Ipoh High Court recovery order which compelled police to find Indira's daughter, Prasana Diksa, and arrest her ex-husband, Muhammad Riduan Abdullah. Indira's lawyer, Rajesh Nagarajan, told the New Straits Times that despite many years, the court order has yet to be excuted.

items.title: 1.5 million vehicles expected to enter Penang during Hari Raya
items.summary: State police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the projection covered the duration of Op Selamat 26, which was conducted from March 19 to March 24. Azizee said the operation was aimed at ensuring road users arrived safely at their destinations while homes left behind remained secure under police monitoring. Meanwhile, Azizee said police had identified several accident-prone hotspots and blackspots across the state, including Jalan Teluk Air Tawar, Jalan Perusahaan Prai, Jalan Besar Simpang Empat (Km12 to Km32), Lebuhraya Tun Dr Lim Chong Eu, Jalan Air Itam, Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah, Jalan Tun Dr Awang and Butterworth Outer Ring Road (BORR).

items.title: Budget hotels not expecting increase in bookings for Hari Raya
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Budget hotels across Malaysia are facing a challenging Hari Raya season, with high cost of living, stagnant room rates and unlicensed accommodations that continue to affect their profit margins. Malaysia Budget and Business Hotel Association (MyBHA) president Dr Sri Ganesh Michiel said the traditional "balik kampung" rush no longer guarantees an immediate windfall for the budget hotel sector. Ganesh said there is a noticeable trend in booking patterns, where budget hotels are often the secondary choice for families traveling back to their hometowns.

items.title: Does the AG have immunity? Najib’s launches final bid to revive suit against Tommy Thomas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak has taken his RM1.9 million lawsuit against former attorney general Tan Sri Tommy Thomas to the Federal Court in a final bid to revive the case. The move comes after the Court of Appeal in January upheld a High Court decision to strike out the suit, which alleges misfeasance in public office and malicious prosecution, Free Malaysia Today reported Najib sued Thomas in 2020 over charges brought against him related to 1MDB, seeking RM1.9 million in special damages to cover his legal defence preparation costs. In a significant finding, however, the appellate court disagreed with the High Court on one key point: the immunity of the attorney general.

items.title: TNB steps up preparations for Aidilfitri power demand
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) has made comprehensive preparations to ensure a stable and continuous electricity supply during this year's Aidilfitri celebrations. As part of its readiness plan, TNB has implemented several electricity network upgrade projects nationwide, while continuously monitoring its network and critical assets. As a further mitigation measure, TNB has also carried out load balancing initiatives, increased transformer capacity and enhanced its electricity network system to ensure stable distribution throughout the festive period.

items.title: Indonesian man charged over student's death in sickle-linked crash
items.summary: TELUK INTAN: An Indonesian man was charged in the magistrate's court here today with two offences for riding a motorcycle dangerously, resulting in the death of a vocational college student and injuring another teenager in an incident last week. According to the first charge, Sutiyono, who was riding a Honda EX5 motorcycle, was accused of riding in a manner dangerous to the public, causing the death of 18-year-old Adham Faisal. For the second charge, Sutiyono was accused of riding dangerously, causing injury to 17-year-old Muhammad Mu'ammar Ismail at the same place, date and time.

items.title: Tabung Haji declares 3.5pct payout for FY25, highest in eight years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) has declared a higher profit distribution of 3.50 per cent after zakat for the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025 (FY25), compared with 3.25 per cent in FY24, marking its highest payout in eight years. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan said the distribution involves RM3.22 billion and will benefit more than 9.7 million TH depositors. "The payout is higher than the 3.25 per cent recorded for 2024, reflecting stronger financial performance and disciplined financial management," he said at the announcement.

items.title: Ringgit climbs to five-year high vs Singapore dollar amid Middle East tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit climbed to the strongest versus the Singapore dollar in five years amid cautious sentiment surrounding the Middle East conflict. On Wednesday, the local currency strengthened 0.2 per cent to 3.0615 versus its Singaporean counterpart, a level last seen in March 2021. As at 9am, the ringgit further strengthened to 3.0605 against the Singapore dollar.

items.title: Storm damages over 50 homes in Melaka ahead of Aidilfitri
items.summary: MELAKA: More than 50 houses in several areas around Bukit Rambai here were damaged following a storm and strong winds at 6pm yesterday. Klebang state assemblyman Lim Ban Hong said the affected areas included Kampung Gedong Lalang, Seberang Gajah, Taman Bukit Rambai and Rumah Awam Fatimah. He said the incident caused various types of property damage to residents' homes, including roofs and ceilings being torn off, while some houses were struck by fallen trees.

items.title: Petronas highlights Sabah's expanding role in Malaysia's oil and gas sector
items.summary: With the support and ongoing collaboration with Sabah, we can build a resilient OGSE supply chain that can strengthen Sabah's position as a competitive destination for energy sector investments," he said during the Commemoration of Sabah OGSE Vendors here recently. Bacho said strengthening Sabah's OGSE ecosystem is essential in ensuring Malaysia's continued competitiveness in the regional and global energy sector. Petronas and the Sabah state government also recognised Sabah-based OGSE vendors' contribution in fostering a sustainable and competitive local industry ecosystem.

items.title: Customs detect firework smuggling via lorries and boats at illegal crossings
items.summary: Director-general Datuk Amran Ahmad said cases of smuggling and storing fireworks without a valid permit continue to rise ahead of celebrations to meet market demand. He said one method involves importers with permits making false declarations, with imported firecrackers differing from the approved specifications. "Secondly, unapproved firecrackers and fireworks are brought in alongside those with valid permits.

items.title: Free Go KL bus to Cheras Rehab Hospital from April 1, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh today announced that the free Go KL bus service will be extended to the Cheras Rehabilitation Hospital (HRC) from April 1, in a move aimed at improving access to public healthcare, particularly for the elderly and those with mobility challenges. She said Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) will expand the GOKL11 route – which runs between MRT Maluri and Bandar Tun Razak – by adding a new stop within the hospital grounds. She said the bus service will provide much needed access to the public.

items.title: Grab launches '5-Star Eats' food guide for VMY2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Grab Malaysia, in strategic partnership with Tourism Malaysia, has officially launched '5-Star Eats', an in-app food guide designed to be the premier culinary compass for Visit Malaysia Year 2026 (VMY2026). PROMOTING REAL-WORLD HERITAGE DIGITALLY To bridge the gap between digital discovery and the city's vibrant food scene, Grab commissioned a landmark 5-Star Eats Mural at Jalan Hang Kasturi. SPOTLIGHTING LOCAL 5-STAR LEGENDS The guide elevates local small and medium-sized businesses, positioning them as essential culinary destinations.

items.title: Police probe theft of 30 urns from Nilai memorial park
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have confirmed another case involving stolen urns at a memorial park in Nilai. Nilai district police chief, Superintendent Johari Yahya, said a total of 30 columbarium urns were reported stolen from a cemetery in Nilai, adding that the cemetery management had lodged a police report. He said police received the report on Feb 26, with the complainant claiming that several urns had gone missing from a columbarium at the cemetery.

items.title: Penang JPJ ramps up Raya patrols in road safety push, aims to cut accidents by 10pc
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, March 18 — The Penang Road Transport Department (JPJ) is intensifying its advocacy programmes during Ops Hari Raya Aidilfitri (HRA) 2026, aiming to reduce road accidents by 10 per cent throughout the festive season. State JPJ director Zulkifly Ismail said more face-to-face engagements with drivers are being carried out to boost the Perception of Being Caught (POBC) among road users. He said eight key areas have been identified as frequent accident zones, including Jalan Teluk Air Tawar to Jalan Bagan Luar and the North-South Expressway in North Seberang Perai; Jalan Perusahaan Perai in Central Seberang Perai; Jalan Besar Simpang Ampat in South Seberang Perai; Tun Dr Lim Chong Eu Expressway and Jalan Paya Terubong in the Northeast; as well as Jalan Tun Dr Awang and Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah in the Southwest.

items.title: Iran army vows revenge for security chief Larijani's killing
items.summary: Tehran, March 18, 2026 (AFP) - Iranian army chief Amir Hatami threatened on Wednesday to launch a "decisive and regrettable" retaliation for the killing of security chief Ali Larijani in an Israeli air strike. "Iran's response to the assassination of the secretary of the Supreme National Security Council will be decisive and regrettable," Hatami said in a statement. The Revolutionary Guards, Iran's powerful military force that is separate from the army, said in a statement Wednesday that it had launched missiles at central Israel "in revenge for the blood of martyr Dr Ali Larijani and his companions."

items.title: Tick-tock trouble: Domestic trade officers seize knock-off luxe watches worth nearly RM800,000 in Putrajaya
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 18 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) seized 790 units of counterfeit branded watches, worth an estimated RM790,600, during a raid in the federal capital on March 13. KPDN Enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said the raid was conducted by the Putrajaya Enforcement Division following a nearly month-long investigation into premises suspected of storing and distributing the fake watches. Azman said the counterfeit watches were believed to have been sourced from overseas suppliers before being sold to local customers.

items.title: MBSB IB: High oil prices may weigh on property sector
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's property sector could face a mild downturn if elevated oil prices persist, as higher fuel costs drive up transportation expenses, leading to increased construction costs for developers, said MBSB Investment Bank Bhd (MBSB IB). MBSB IB expects the rising property overhang to be a growing concern to the sector, while the oil price hike might derail the growth of property demand. "However, we remain positive on property sector as the near-term prospect for property demand should remain healthy.

items.title: D&O Green Technologies set for smart LED sales boost on higher EV demand
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: D&O Green Technologies Bhd is poised to see its smart LED sales double to between 15 and 18 per cent this year, said Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest Research). The firm said the growth in sales will be driven by higher demand from BMW and a new customer base in China and South Korea. "On a positive note, BMW's tier-1 module maker's sales now account for 15-18 per cent of its group sales, up from nine per cent previously," said the firm.

items.title: Gold steady as investors weigh Mideast risks ahead of Fed decision
items.summary: NEW YORK: Gold prices held steady on Wednesday as investors stayed on the sidelines, assessing the economic impact of the Middle East conflict ahead of the US Federal Reserve's policy decision. Spot gold edged down 0.1 per cent at US$5,000.77 per ounce as of 0243 GMT. US gold futures for April delivery fell 0.1 per cent to US$5,004.60.

items.title: Anwar wins bid to strike out legal challenge over PM and Tambun MP posts, judge rules Waytha Moorthy has no case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The High Court here today allowed Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s bid to strike out an originating summons filed by P. Waytha Moorthy challenging his appointment as Prime Minister and Tambun Member of Parliament (MP). Judge Alice Loke Yee Ching, in allowing the application, held that the court has no jurisdiction to entertain the summons, as any challenge to an election must be brought by way of an election petition. Justice Loke further said that the court, in hearing the originating summons, was not sitting as an election court, nor was the action commenced by way of an election petition.

items.title: MARKET PULSE AM MARCH 18, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's 30-stock index opened higher, mirroring Wall Street's rally. The key index rose three points at the opening bell to 1,713. YHWL4BR5OZc © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Samsung Elec plans to produce Tesla chips starting late 2027
items.summary: SEOUL: Samsung Electronics said on Wednesday that it expects to start volume production of Tesla's chips at Samsung's factory in Texas in the second half of next year. The comments were made by Han Jin-man, Samsung Electronics President and Head of Foundry Business, at a shareholders' meeting. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Family of five injured after car plunges into drain in Kota Tinggi
items.summary: The 2.07pm incident involved three adults and two children, with two victims trapped in the wreckage. "Upon arrival, rescuers found two victims trapped inside the vehicle, while three others had been extricated earlier with the help of members of the public," he said in a statement. Firefighters freed the trapped victims before all five were taken to Kota Tinggi Hospital for treatment.

items.title: Perak deploys 255 traffic personnel to monitor Hari Raya roads and blackspots
items.summary: GOPENG: Perak police have deployed 255 traffic personnel to monitor 48 congested routes and 29 accident-prone locations during Op Selamat 26 ahead of Hari Raya. The first phase is Thursday and Friday, with the second phase from March 27 to March 29, aiming to ease traffic and reduce accidents. During Op Selamat 24 last year, Perak recorded 960 accidents, including 10 fatalities.

items.title: Hire-purchase changes aim to protect borrowers, little impact on banks, says economist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The introduction of key amendments to hire-purchase financing practices under the Hire-Purchase (Amendment) Act 2026 (HPAA) marks a significant move in strengthening borrowers’ protection, particularly for those looking to sell vehicles before their financing contracts mature, an economist said. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the changes would benefit borrowers who intend to trade in or dispose of their vehicles early, as the reducing balance method offers greater savings than the Rule of 78, which front-loads interest at the beginning of the loan tenure. He said to some degree, it could help improve purchasers’ affordability.

items.title: Nikkei jumps nearly 2pct as AI, chip stocks rally
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's Nikkei share average climbed nearly two per cent on Wednesday, led by chip and artificial intelligence (AI)-related stocks, as concerns over the economic impact of surging oil prices subsided. The Nikkei was up 1.91 per cent at 54,726.58, as of 0140 GMT, while the broader Topix rose 1.66 per cent to 3,687.35. In Japan, chip-testing equipment maker Advantest jumped 4.95 per cent and AI-technology investor SoftBank Group rose five per cent.

items.title: Asian stocks rally as oil retreats, Fed in spotlight
items.summary: Brent crude futures dropped one per cent to US$102.28 a barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate crude fell 1.6 per cent. Japan's Nikkei rallied two per cent. Chinese blue-chips inched up 0.1 per cent while Hong Kong's Hang Seng index rose 0.3 per cent.

items.title: Malaysia, a net oil importer, remains exposed to global price threats
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Changes in the global energy market structure mean Malaysia, long known as an oil and gas producing nation, remains directly exposed to volatility in global prices, especially amid tensions between the United States and Iran. Petronas Dagangan Bhd managing director and chief executive officer Azrul Osman Rani said Malaysia is now effectively a net importer of crude oil. Azrul said although the country still holds the status of an oil and gas producer, the structure of the energy market means consumers remain directly tied to fluctuations in global prices.

items.title: Misuse of public donation funds worsens amid rise in MACC probes [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Misuse of public funds, including donations and charitable contributions, has become more serious following a rise in cases involving lack of transparency and the use of funds for personal gain. Apart from NGOs, individuals, including social media influencers, were also found to have actively collected donations and charitable funds from the public, purportedly for welfare purposes. "Many think public funds only involve government money, but NGOs that misuse donations from the public can also face action.

items.title: Spoofed email scam sees accountant transfer over RM700k to fake supplier
items.summary: TANGKAK: It began with a routine order and ended in a six-figure loss after a company here was duped by a business email scam that siphoned off RM704,234.58 within a short time. Tangkak district police chief, Superintendent Roslan Mohd Talib, said the victim had been communicating with a suspect via email over a purported steel supply transaction. He said the business email account had been compromised, allowing the scammer to exploit the situation.

items.title: Accidental tick? Marking error won’t hold up 2025 income tax refunds, IRB assures taxpayers
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 18 — Individual taxpayers do not need to submit an amended Income Tax Return Form (BNCP) for the Year of Assessment 2025 to correct an accidental error in marking the “Tax Borne by Employer” column. “The IRB has noted that some individual taxpayers inadvertently marked the ‘Tax borne by employer’ column when filling out their Income Tax Return Forms for the Year of Assessment 2025, resulting in delays in receiving excess tax refunds. “For cases confirmed as marking errors, the excess tax refunds will be processed in stages,” the statement said The IRB explained that the tax refund system operates automatically based on the information provided by taxpayers in their returns.

items.title: Putra Heights blast: Painful memories haunt residents ahead of Hari Raya
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA: As Hari Raya approaches, the usual anticipation in Kampung Kuala Sungai Baru, Putra Heights, is tinged with unease. For residents, the festival now carries painful memories of shattered homes and disrupted lives. Seventy-year-old former contractor Mohamad Nasir Abd Hamid still returns to the ruins of his house.

items.title: ‘Balik kampung’ traffic from Klang Valley: East coast-bound jams start early
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Traffic from the Klang Valley heading to the east coast began to build up this morning as travellers started returning to their hometowns ahead of Aidilfitri. “LLM expects traffic volume to increase further from midday following the 50 per cent toll discount, which began at midnight and will run until 11.59 pm tomorrow,” he told Bernama when contacted. — Bernama

items.title: Japanese firms offer strong pay hikes but Middle East tensions cloud outlook
items.summary: TOKYO: Many major companies are set to offer big pay hikes as they wrap up their annual wage talks with unions on Wednesday, reflecting strong pay momentum for a fourth consecutive year, although uncertainty from the Middle East conflict clouds the outlook. Annual wage talks between management and labour unions typically conclude around mid-March at major firms, with many, including Toyota Motor and Hitachi, finalising their negotiations on Wednesday. Mitsubishi Motors agreed to an average 5.1 per cent pay hike on Feb 25, concluding its annual labour talks at the earliest point since its founding in 1970.

items.title: [UPDATED] High Court rejects Waytha Moorthy's challenge to Anwar as prime minister
items.summary: High Court judge Alice Loke Yee Ching dismissed Waytha's originating summons filed last year, ruling the court lacked jurisdiction, as the suit should have been filed as an election petition. An election can only be challenged through an election petition. "This court, hearing an originating summons, is not an election court, nor is this an election petition.

items.title: New-hire purchase rules to benefit borrowers, minimal impact on banks: economists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The introduction of key amendments to hire-purchase financing practices under the Hire-Purchase (Amendment) Act 2026 (HPAA) marks a significant move in strengthening borrowers' protection, particularly for those looking to sell vehicles before their financing contracts mature, an economist said. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the changes would benefit borrowers who intend to trade in or dispose of their vehicles early, as the reducing balance method offers greater savings than the Rule of 78, which front-loads interest at the beginning of the loan tenure. He said to some degree, it could help improve purchasers' affordability.

items.title: Nightmare in Kampung Segaliud: Father helpless as crocodile drags son away in Kinabatangan, search ongoing
items.summary: Kinabatangan Fire and Rescue Station chief Hairi Otuh said Mohd Rayyan Abdullah, 28, was attacked while casting a net for prawns in the river. Hairi said the rescue team arrived at the location at 9.10 pm to gather information and immediately launched a search and rescue operation. — Bernama

items.title: Middle East tensions, fuel spikes fail to dent Capital A earnings outlook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Escalating tensions in the Middle East and rising jet fuel costs are unlikely to derail Capital A Bhd's earnings trajectory, underscoring the resilience of its diversified business model, according to Maybank Investment Bank (Maybank IB). Yet, Capital A stated that its shareholders' equity will still be positive at RM610 million, or 107 per cent of its share capital," Maybank IB said. Maybank IB said that even after adjusting for the weaker AAX valuation, Maybank IB estimates Capital A's sum-of-parts target price at 65 sen, still implying more than 40 per cent upside.

items.title: Works Ministry’s Raya Gift: A complete AwAS camera cheat sheet (for responsible driving)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Ministry of Works has issued a public reminder to motorists travelling for the Hari Raya Aidilfitri ‘balik kampung’ exodus, releasing a list of Automated Awareness Safety System (AwAS) camera locations along the North-South Expressway (PLUS). Below are the official AwAS camera locations for motorists’ reference. AwAS camera locations on PLUS Expressway Northbound: KM 1, Johor Bahru KM 151.4, Pagoh KM 184.2, Jasin KM 214.4, Alor Gajah KM 299.9, Kampar KM 301.6, Kajang KM 375.9, Slim River KM 204.6, Taiping KM 174, Bandar Baharu KM 97.2, Kuala Muda Southbound: KM 96.3, Kuala Muda KM 166, Seberang Perai Selatan KM 214.4, Alor Gajah KM 328.8, Behrang KM 185, Bemban KM 146.8, Pagoh KM 1, Johor Bahru

items.title: Higher jet fuel prices from Gulf conflict could hurt Sabah tourism comeback, industry leader warns
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 18 — Tourism industry leader Datuk Dr Tan Kok Liang warned that the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran will inevitably disrupt global and domestic travel patterns, specifically threatening Sabah’s tourism sector. Dr Tan also The emphasised that Sabah’s industry, while diversified, remains vulnerable to global shocks. Currently, he said domestic arrivals make up 60 per cent of visitors, followed by China (18 per cent), Asean (11 per cent), South Korea (5 per cent), and the UK/Europe (3 per cent).

items.title: As Petronas heads to Federal Court, state deputy minister says Sarawak ready to file own petition to protect Petros
items.summary: KUCHING, March 18 — The Sarawak government is prepared to file its own petition in the Federal Court to ensure all legal issues surrounding the regulatory framework of Petroleum Sarawak Berhad (Petros) are thoroughly examined, said Deputy Minister in the Sarawak Premier’s Department (Law, MA63 and State-Federal Relations), Datuk Sharifah Hasidah Sayeed Aman Ghazali. Petronas is challenging the legislative competency of the Sarawak State Legislature to enact certain laws and to declare several laws made by the Sarawak State Legislature invalid. Some of the provisions include Sections 7, 7A and 8 of the Sarawak Distribution of Gas Ordinance 2016, Section 3 of the Sarawak Interpretation Ordinance 2005, Section 2 of the Sarawak Oil Mining Ordinance 1958, Section 2 of the Sarawak National Parks and Nature Reserves Ordinance 1998 and Section 2 of the Sarawak Land Code 1958.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng moots tax moratorium, interest-free loans to help MSMEs in ‘crisis of survival’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — DAP advisor Lim Guan Eng today pressed the government to implement urgent measures to help micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) that he said are facing a “crisis of survival” due to the ongoing Middle East conflict. To contain costs and protect businesses, Lim proposed a moratorium on new financial burdens, which would include suspending increases in the Sales and Service Tax (SST), halting the mandatory e-invoicing implementation, and pausing the 2 per cent Employees Provident Fund (EPF) contribution for foreign workers. “The government must realise that MSMEs are facing a real crisis of survival with the ongoing Middle East conflict,” he concluded.

items.title: Man arrested for suspected rape of 14-year-old after ‘khalwat’ raid in Muar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Police have arrested a man in his 20s for the suspected rape of a 14-year-old girl, after the pair were found together in a hotel room during a raid by the Johor Islamic Religious Department (JAINJ) last week. Muar District Police Chief ACP Raizmukhliz Azman Aziz confirmed the arrest in a statement yesterday. “The raid found a man in his 20s together with a girl believed to be approximately 14 years of age inside a hotel room under suspicious circumstances,” Raizmukhliz stated.

items.title: Ahmad Maslan: Umno welcomes back former members while other parties expel theirs
items.summary: KUCHING, March 18 — The ‘Rumah Bangsa’ initiative by Umno is viewed as an extraordinary approach to strengthen the party in the Peninsular and Sabah, said Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan. Meanwhile, Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi had previously said that the ‘Rumah Bangsa’ initiative was open to all without any conditions, including former Umno leaders. Former Umno senior leaders Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and Khairy Jamaludin are reported to have submitted their application letters to rejoin the party Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dasuki, in a post on Facebook Monday, confirmed that former Umno leaders Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and Khairy Jamaludin had submitted application letters to rejoin the party.

items.title: ‘Balik kampung’ in Penang? Don’t miss out on free ferry rides this Aidilfitri
items.summary: Penang Port Commission (SPPP) chairman Datuk Yeoh Soon Hin said that the free ferry fare is a manifestation of their ongoing commitment, along with Penang Port Sdn Bhd (PPSB), to facilitate the travel of the people, especially during the festive season. In line with the initiative to provide free ferry services and the 70th anniversary celebration of SPPP (Platinum Jubilee) this year, Yeoh said SPPP is also sponsoring the implementation of community programmes through the “Kids Go Free|Aidilfitri Special” campaign at the Penang Ferry Museum here. He also said the programme allows Malaysian children, aged 12 years old and below, to enjoy free admission to the ferry museum from March 23 to 25.

items.title: Raya scam alert: How Perak ‘kuih’ sellers lost RM300,000 to fake food orders
items.summary: Perak police chief Datuk Noor Hisam Noordin said the suspects’ modus operandi was to initially place orders in certain quantities with bakeries or catering businesses by giving them time to prepare the orders. “After that, the suspects would request additional items, usually ones not produced by the business operator. “Some business operators made payments.

items.title: J-KOM tells Malaysians to brace for economic impact as US-Iran war rages on
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Malaysians must be prepared to adopt a modest lifestyle amid global uncertainty as international conflicts begin to exert pressure on the domestic economy. Community Communications Department (J-KOM) strategic adviser Datuk Dr Budiman Mohd Zohdi said the current situation demands public preparedness, even though these conflicts do not originate from within the country. Budiman said that in dealing with such instability, people need to shift their mindset by managing their personal finances as if they were managing a ‘small government’.

items.title: Six-person Myvi hit by BMW in fatal Kelantan crash, two dead
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 18 — Two people were killed in a road accident involving two vehicles at Bandar Baru Tanah Merah along Jalan Machang-Tanah Merah early this morning. Tanah Merah Fire and Rescue Station operations commander Senior Fire Officer Hamdan Abdullah said the victims were a 64-year-old woman and a 14-year-old girl. “Two victims, who were passengers in the Perodua Myvi, died at the scene, while the others survived,” he said when contacted by Bernama today.

items.title: Why Malaysian youths can be vulnerable to extremist recruitment and how radicalisation takes root
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The recent arrests of six youths allegedly linked to extremist plots have renewed questions about how young people become vulnerable to radical ideologies and extremist recruitment. Digital platforms may play a role in spreading extremist propaganda, but the factors that make youths vulnerable to extremist messaging often started long before they even came across on-screen content. Radicalisation does not follow a single pathway Andrin Raj, director of the Nordic Counter Terrorism Network (NCTN), Finland, said the pathways that shape extremist beliefs among youths can vary widely depending on local cultural, political and religious environments.

items.title: ‘Why would I meet Anwar? In Parliament, is that wrong?’ Hamzah hits back at claims of secret meetings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 17 — Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainuddin has hit back at allegations that he frequently met Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim after being sacked from Bersatu. The Larut MP also said that Datuk Seri Azmin Ali has been purported to have regularly met Anwar and challenged the Bersatu secretary-general to clarify the allegations, Berita Harian reported this evening. “Why would I meet Anwar?

items.title: PMO sends RM300,000 Ramadan gifts to 650 frontline service officers
items.summary: MELAKA, March 17 — The Prime Minister’s Office (PMO) has allocated RM300,000 to implement the Juadah Ramadan Madani: Frontline Appreciation initiative, as a sincere gesture and gift from the Prime Minister to frontline personnel dedicated to their duties during this holy month. The Prime Minister’s political secretary, Chan Ming Kai, said Melaka was the final state in the series of iftar package distributions carried out today, with 650 police officers and personnel being the recipients. Also present were the director of the Implementation Coordination Unit, Prime Minister’s Department, Mohd Shahrul Shari, and Melaka police management department head Supt Nik Abdul Halim Nik Mat.

items.title: Poultry in motion: PM Anwar contributes 14,000 chickens for Raya, reaches 29 mosques in Permatang Pauh
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 17 — A total of 14,000 chickens were distributed to residents in the Permatang Pauh parliamentary constituency through 29 mosques in the area as preparation to celebrate Hari Raya Aidilfitri. Permatang Pauh Madani People’s Service Centre officer Ahmad Zhafri Mohd Nazri said the contribution is an initiative by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, in collaboration with the National Action Council on Cost of Living (Naccol). Yayasan Aman provided 400 chickens, and the distribution was completed within 15 minutes.

items.title: Ticket to ride: KTMB goes near 24-hour to meet Aidilfitri travel rush
items.summary: Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the increase is driven by the additional 228 extra train trips from March 17 to 29, a 113 per cent increase compared to the 118,440 seats provided last year. “The increase in ETS services this year is also supported by the delivery of 10 ETS3 train sets, along with existing ETS sets, enabling KTMB to optimise service capacity to facilitate people returning to their hometowns,” he said after meeting passengers and distributing 500 Hari Raya gift packs to KTMB passengers at KL Sentral today. “As a comparison, we added up to 196 additional ETS trips this year compared to only 56 last year, an increase of nearly 250 per cent.

items.title: Raya road trip countdown: 3.5 million vehicles set to roll, smart lanes and discounts ready from March 18
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 17 — The Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) expects traffic of over 3.49 million vehicles on major highways and connecting expressways during peak travel days for the Aidilfitri celebrations. Major highways like the PLUS Group is expected to record 2.30 million vehicles, followed by the West Coast Expressway (WCE) (215,000); the KL-Karak Highway (210,000); the East Coast Expressway Phase 1 (106,000); and Phase 2 (65,000), he added. “Meanwhile, for connecting highways, the Duta-Ulu Kelang Expressway (Duke) is projected to record 295,000 vehicles, the Kajang-Seremban Expressway (Lekas) 159,000, and the Kuala Lumpur-Kuala Selangor Expressway (Latar) 135,000 vehicles,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Johor Regent eyes residential project near Singapore Botanic Gardens
items.summary: SINGAPORE, March 17 — The Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, plans to develop residential properties on his land parcels at Holland Road following a land swap with the Singapore government announced last year. A media release published on the Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA) website stated that it had received a development application from the Regent of Johor to develop the land parcels for residential use. In June 2025, a land swap deal was agreed, under which Singapore received a 13-hectare (ha) plot of land abutting the Singapore Botanic Gardens, while Tunku Mahkota Ismail received an 8.5-ha piece of state land in exchange.

items.title: Download the app first: West Coast Expressway braces for Raya rush from March 20–29, but some sections yet to open
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 17 — West Coast Expressway Holdings Berhad (WCEHB) expects more than 200,000 vehicles to use the West Coast Expressway (WCE) daily from March 20 to 29, following a sharp increase in traffic in conjunction with the long Hari Raya Aidilfitri holidays. WCE Strategic Communications and Stakeholder Engagement General Manager Syed Mohammed Idid said the surge reflects high travel demand based on recent festive patterns, where the highway previously recorded between 160,000 and 170,000 vehicles per day. “WCE remains committed to ensuring safe and efficient journeys for all road users,” he said in a statement today, as he advised highway users to plan their trips early to avoid congestion, especially during peak hours expected in the afternoon until night.

items.title: Out of the country, out of court: No-show Tamim Dahri delays sacrilege charge to May 17, whereabouts unconfirmed
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 17 — Activist Tamim Dahri Abdul Razak failed to turn up at the Magistrate’s Court in Langkawi today where he was to be charged with defiling an object sacred to Hindus. Kedah Director of Prosecutions Khairul Anuar Abdul Halim said more time was needed to secure the 34-year-old, who is currently believed to be out of the country, national news agency Bernama reported. He said Tamim is expected to be charged under Section 295 of the Penal Code, which pertains to damaging or desecrating objects held sacred by any religious group with the intent to insult religion.

items.title: Johor Bomba honours holidaying dad for saving two kids from drowning at Kota Tinggi
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 17 — The bravery of a father who saved two young victims from drowning at Pantai Batu Layar, Kota Tinggi, on Jan 1 has been recognised by the Johor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM). Mohamad Danial Md Sanin, 36, said he was on holiday with his family when he heard cries for help coming from the beach. Johor JBPM director Siti Rohani Nadir said that besides Mohamad Danial, several other individuals who assisted the fire department in its daily duties also received certificates of appreciation.

items.title: Miti to continue engaging US on trade after ART voided, says Fahmi
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 17 — The government will continue to engage with the US on a trade framework, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said today, clarifying the path forward after a previous agreement was rendered void. Speaking after a Cabinet meeting today, Fahmi confirmed that Minister of Investment, Trade and Industry (MITI) Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani will lead the ongoing talks with the US. The minister of investment, trade and industry will continue to engage with Washington DC,” Fahmi told a press conference here.
